(June 9, 6:18 am ADT) - Dennis Madsen plans to retire as chief executive of Recreational Equipment Inc. and will be replaced in March by Sally Jewell, currently chief operating officer of the outdoor retail cooperative. Madsen, 55, an avid cyclist, skier and mountain climber who started working at REI as a stock clerk at age 17, said Tuesday he decided to leave because "there's a lot of things I want to do, and what better time to leave this company?" title: CIRI board gets a different look
summary: Shareholders of Cook Inlet Region Inc. have elected three incumbents and two reformers to the CIRI board of the directors. The election results, announced at Saturday's annual meeting, mean the Alliance for the Future of CIRI, a group critical of the company's management, now controls six seats on the 15-member board. While the outcome doesn't mean a sea change in the balance of power, it shows the Alliance campaign, launched in 2002, continues to gain momentum, a prospect that troubles those running one of Alaska's most successful Native corporations. "It's a big win for the Alliance," said Tom Huhndorf, an Anchorage businessman who was elected on the Alliance slate. summary: Alaskans able to switch cell service without losing number The Associated Press ANCHORAGE (May 23, 2:36 pm ADT) - Starting this week, Alaskans will be able to keep their cell phone numbers even if they switch phone companies. New federal rules that allow the change take effect here Monday. Cell phone users in the country's 100 largest cities have been able to switch companies without losing their numbers since late November. Wireless local number portability had a rough start in the Lower 48 as cell phone companies ran into technical glitches while adjusting to new procedures they had to put in place to make it work. summary: Since 1824, under this Rotunda, our nation has paid final tribute to many dedicated public servants. President Abraham Lincoln was the first president to lie in state under this Capitol dome. In the coming days, thousands will come to these hallowed halls to say goodbye to another son of Illinois who, like Lincoln, appealed to our best hopes, not our worst fears. In the life of any nation, few men forever alter the course of history. Ronald Reagan was one of those men.
